scenarios.input: A SaaS vendor provides a customer portal for accessing financial reports. Multi-factor authentication is not supported. Users authenticate with username and password only. The platform stores customer financial data.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25. Total=25. Rating: Medium (21-50). Baseline single-control IAM failure.

scenarios.input: A project management tool requires employees to maintain separate login credentials. No SAML or OAuth SSO integration is available. The platform does not handle sensitive or regulated data.
scenarios.notes: sso_missing=10. Total=10. Rating: Low (0-20). Tests that SSO alone does not inflate rating to Medium.

scenarios.input: A cloud document sharing platform used for sharing confidential vendor contracts offers neither MFA nor SSO. Users maintain standalone accounts with password-only authentication.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, sso_missing=10. Total=35. Rating: Medium. Tests additive scoring and dual control detection.

scenarios.input: A payment processing vendor handles retail transaction data. Admin accounts do not require MFA. Transaction records are stored unencrypted at rest on cloud storage servers.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, no_encryption=30. Total=55. Rating: High. First High scenario — crosses Medium/High boundary. Tests cross-domain source citation.

scenarios.input: A healthcare analytics vendor processing de-identified patient data does not enforce MFA on user accounts. The last penetration test was conducted 20 months ago and no test is currently scheduled.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, no_annual_pentest=20. Total=45. Rating: Medium. Two significant failures that remain Medium — catches hallucination that inflates to High.

scenarios.input: A cloud backup vendor storing customer data cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 evidence. MFA is also not implemented for user accounts accessing the backup console.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=40. Rating: Medium. Tests Compliance Mapping Guide source citation alongside MFA.

scenarios.input: A CRM vendor managing customer contact data including email addresses and phone numbers does not support MFA. The vendor has no documented incident response plan and cannot provide evidence of IR testing or tabletop exercises.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, no_ir_plan=15. Total=40. Rating: Medium. Tests Incident Response Policy source citation.

scenarios.input: A legal technology vendor stores confidential litigation documents and client contracts. No MFA is enforced on any user account. No SSO integration is available. Files are stored without encryption at rest on the vendor's servers.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, sso_missing=10, no_encryption=30. Total=65. Rating: High. Tests 3-control detection (FM-005) and cross-domain sources.

scenarios.input: A third-party API provider processes customer PII for analytics reporting. MFA is not required on any account. SSO is not supported. No annual penetration test has been conducted in the past two years. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 documentation.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, sso_missing=10, no_annual_pentest=20, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=70. Rating: High. Complex 4-control, 3-source scenario. Primary test for FM-005 incomplete coverage.

scenarios.input: An IT service management vendor runs a help desk ticketing system that handles employee personal data. MFA is not enforced for any user. SSO integration is unavailable. The vendor has no formal incident response plan documented or tested.
scenarios.notes: mfa_missing=25, sso_missing=10, no_ir_plan=15. Total=50. Rating: MEDIUM — score is exactly at the Medium ceiling. Any response of High is a business accuracy failure. Designed to catch rating boundary errors.

scenarios.input: A market data vendor provides read-only financial data feeds. MFA is enforced for all user accounts. SSO via SAML 2.0 is available and configured. Data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Annual penetration tests are completed with reports provided on request. SOC2 Type II certified. A documented incident response plan is in place with quarterly tabletop exercises. Data retention policies are documented and enforced.
scenarios.notes: No controls failing. Total=0. Rating: Low. Tests graceful handling of a compliant vendor. failed_controls must be empty. Catches models that hallucinate failures or refuse to return Low ratings.

scenarios.sub_type: Edge case — all controls failing, score capped
scenarios.input: A payroll processing vendor managing salary and tax data for all employees has failed every security control in the assessment. The vendor has no MFA, no SSO, no data encryption at rest, no annual penetration test, no incident response plan, no compliance certifications, no data retention policy, and critical CVE exposures have been identified on production servers.
scenarios.notes: All 8 controls fail. Raw sum=25+10+30+20+15+15+10+30=155. Capped at 100. Rating: Critical. Tests score capping, all-control detection, all-source citation, and model stability under maximum failure load.
